title: Styling in JSX
description: Apply inline styles and CSS classes.

slides.title: Two Ways to Style 🎨
slides.content:
# Inline Styles vs CSS Classes ## 1. Inline Styles Use the `style` attribute with a JavaScript object. Property names are camelCased. ```jsx
Hello
``` ## 2. CSS Classes Use the `className` attribute (not `class`). ```jsx
Hello
```

slides.title: Styling Example
slides.content: Here's a component using both methods:

quizData.question: In JSX, you use `class` instead of `className` to apply CSS classes.
quizData.explanation: False. `class` is a reserved word in JavaScript, so React uses `className`.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Dynamic Classes

You can combine strings or use template literals to set classes conditionally:

```jsx
<div className={`base ${isActive ? 'active' : ''}`}>
```

Or use the `clsx` or `classnames` library for complex logic.
